changesProposed.name: Fervo Energy

verified.name: Fervo Energy
verified.description: Geothermal energy company that generates electricity through enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling techniques adapted from oil and gas technology. The company develops and operates next-generation geothermal projects including Project Red and Cape Station.
verified.notes: Company went public on May 17, 2026 (Nasdaq debut). Wikipedia article notes it was marked 'Private' but this conflicts with source [0] which contains multiple May 17, 2026 newsroom entries stating 'Fervo Energy raises $1.89B in IPO' and 'Geothermal-Champion Fervo Energy's Shares Soar in Trading Debut'. Current stage classified as 'Public' based on IPO announcement. Source [2] indicates Series E funding of $462M in November 2025 prior to IPO.

changesProposed.name: PLD Space

current.name: PLD Space
current.founder: Raúl Torres, Raúl Verdú, José Enrique Martínez

verified.name: PLD Space
verified.description: Spanish launch services provider developing the Miura family of partially-reusable launch vehicles for suborbital and orbital missions. Successfully launched Miura 1 suborbital rocket on 7 October 2023.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states total funding of €120M as of 1 June 2024. Database entry references €180M Series C (March 2026) and €30M EIB venture debt (April 2026), which are not verifiable in provided sources and appear to be future projections. Current stage listed as 'SPAC' in database but not confirmed in sources.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and artificial intelligence company founded in 2002 that develops rocket launch vehicles, spacecraft, and satellite constellation technology. SpaceX conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider as of 2026.
verified.notes: Source [1] states SpaceX 'is expected to have an initial public offering (IPO) in 2026' and notes a 2025 share offer valuation of $800 billion. Company is currently private, not Series G as in database entry. Sources [0] appears to be a domain-for-sale page and is not a reliable source for company information. Sources [2] and [3] are news articles without sufficient detail for verification of specific company metrics.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: A United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. The company launched Starcloud-1 in November 2025 equipped with an NVIDIA H100 GPU.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as 'Lumen Orbit' in January 2024 in El Segundo, California, then rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 following a legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. Became fastest unicorn in Y Combinator history at 17 months after completing the program (March 2026). Series A led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures announced March 30, 2026.

changesProposed.name: Thea Energy

verified.name: Thea Energy
verified.description: American fusion power company developing stellarator technology using planar electromagnetic coils for magnetic confinement fusion. The company is building Eos, a neutron source demonstrator, as an intermediate step toward commercial fusion power plants.
verified.notes: Company was formerly known as Princeton Stellarators, Inc. (PSI). Technology licensed from Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory (PPPL). Series A round announced September 2024. Source [3] (TechCrunch article dated 2026-05-27) mentions $100M raise but is dated in the future relative to verification cutoff and conflicts with Wikipedia source [2] reporting $20M Series A in September 2024; therefore excluded from verified fields.
